channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Jon Stewart Reacts to Trump's Unusual Signature During Psychedelic Drug Executive Order Signing
item.description: During a recent episode of 'The Daily Show,' Jon Stewart highlighted an unusual moment in the Oval Office involving President Donald Trump. While signing an executive order aimed at expediting the rev...

item.title: Family mourns Johannesburg man killed in Emmarentia road rage shooting
item.description: Faisal Ul Rehman, 48, tragically lost his life in a road rage incident on Barry Hertzog Avenue, Emmarentia, Johannesburg, over the past weekend. The altercation began as a minor fender-bender and esca...

item.title: Practical ways Nigerian students can earn money while waiting for university admission
item.description: For many Nigerian students wey don finish exams and dey wait for JAMB or university admission, time fit drag and money no dey. This article show 10 practical ways to earn small small money without deg...

item.title: Australian sharemarket edges lower as energy and gold stocks decline amid US-Iran tensions
item.description: The Australian sharemarket finished almost flat on Tuesday, with the S&P/ASX 200 closing down just 3.9 points at 8949.40. Investors are watching developments in US-Iran peace talks after escalating te...

item.title: Stars Gather for The Devil Wears Prada 2 Premiere in New York
item.description: Anne Hathaway graced the red carpet in New York City for the premiere of 'The Devil Wears Prada 2', looking radiant in a crimson strapless Louis Vuitton gown with a pleated skirt. She reunited with co...

item.title: Kenya Transport Fare Hikes Under Scrutiny Amid Rising Fuel Costs
item.description: The National Taxpayers Association (NTA) has expressed concern over rising public transport fares, cautioning that some operators may be unfairly taking advantage of recent fuel price increases. CEO P...

item.title: Trump grants pardon to nursing home owner amid unresolved family lawsuit
item.description: In a controversial move, former President Donald Trump issued a pardon to Joseph Schwartz, the owner of a nursing home in the United States, who was facing a nearly $19 million judgment stemming from ...

item.title: Court to hear final evidence on alleged police abuse in Lusikisiki mass murder case
item.description: The Mthatha High Court in the Eastern Cape is preparing to hear the final testimony in a trial within a trial linked to the Lusikisiki mass murder case. This legal process is focused on determining wh...

item.title: The decline of tabloid newspapers in the digital era
item.description: The article examines the ongoing decline of traditional tabloid newspapers, arguing that their long-term survival—whether in print or digital form—is increasingly unlikely. Triggered by a recent Finan...

item.title: RSS Chief Mohan Bhagwat Highlights the Importance of Sanskrit for Learning Local Languages
item.description: Rashtriya Swayamsevak Sangh (RSS) chief Mohan Bhagwat, on 20th April 2026, emphasised the significance of Sanskrit as a unifying language across India’s diverse linguistic landscape. Speaking at an ev...

item.title: Justice and Trauma: Addressing Endemic Injustice Across African Nations
item.description: The article explores the deeply rooted challenges facing justice systems across African nations, arguing that endemic injustice is perpetuating cycles of trauma and hindering progress. It highlights t...

item.title: 9 Arrested After BJP-TMC Clash Outside Kolkata Police Station Amid Election Campaigning
item.description: Tensions escalated outside Ekbalpur Police Station in Kolkata on Monday as a clash broke out between supporters of the BJP and the TMC, resulting in the arrest of nine individuals. The incident occurr...

item.title: New KMPDC Board Inaugurated as Kenya Strengthens Health Sector Reforms
item.description: The Ministry of Health in Kenya has officially inaugurated a new board for the Kenya Medical Practitioners and Dentists Council (KMPDC) following the expiry of the previous council's term. The ceremon...

item.title: Nigerian Wrestler Oba Femi Triumphs Over Brock Lesnar at WrestleMania
item.description: Kenya, and indeed the world, witnessed a historic moment on April 19, 2026, as Nigerian wrestler Oba Femi, known in the ring as 'The Ruler', defeated WWE legend Brock Lesnar at WrestleMania 42. The yo...

item.title: Saskatchewan Launches Its First Legal Clinic for Black Communities in Saskatoon
item.description: The Truly Alive Youth And Family Foundation Inc. (TAYFFI) in Saskatoon has launched Saskatchewan's first Black legal clinic, named Leading Change Justice Navigation for Black Adults. The clinic aims t...

item.title: Poilievre's Support Declines Among Conservative Voters: Latest Poll Shows Erosion
item.description: Recent polling by Angus Reid shows that Pierre Poilievre's popularity as the leader of the Conservative Party has decreased, despite maintaining a relatively strong following. The new poll, conducted ...

item.title: How Poor Scope Management Makes Freelancers Feel Undervalued
item.description: This article explains how many freelancers end up feeling underpaid and frustrated due to poor control of project scope. It shows that what starts as a simple job can slowly grow into a heavy workload...

item.title: Rebel Wilson sued for defamation over dispute regarding writing credit on 'The Deb'
item.description: Rebel Wilson is facing a defamation lawsuit brought by Charlotte MacInnes, lead actor in the musical comedy 'The Deb', following a dispute over sexual harassment claims and writing credits. MacInnes f...

item.title: Carney’s messaging offers reassurance, but rising inflation clouds Canada’s economic outlook
item.description: In this opinion piece, John Ivison examines Prime Minister Mark Carney’s recent “fireside chat” video, portraying it as a polished but ultimately insufficient response to Canada’s worsening economic c...

item.title: Uganda and Congo Forces Free 200 Hostages from Islamic State Group Camp
item.description: In a joint military operation, forces from Uganda and the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) rescued over 200 civilians held captive by the Allied Democratic Forces (ADF), an armed group aligned with ...
